title: rubus xanthoneurus var. glandulosus
intro: Rubus xanthoneurus var. glandulosus, also known as the glandular thimbleberry, is a variety of the thimbleberry plant, a type of flowering shrub native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its anthocyanin content, which is known to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Rubus xanthoneurus var. glandulosus include its potential to help protect against cell damage, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional medicine and as a food ingredient, particularly in jams and preserves.
